title: NGEs Biggest Apparent Plothole Solved
wikitext:
[]: NGE's Biggest Apparent Plothole Solved?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Aug 30, 2008 8:18 pm Commonly brought up as one of the biggest blunders in Neon Genesis Evangelion is the fact that Kaworu seems surprised to find the body of Lilith in Terminal Dogma instead of Adam, despite the fact that he was already told by SEELE that Adam's resurrected body exists within Gendo. Kaji also made a similarly bizarre mistake. Since he was the one who delivered the embryo of Adam to Gendo, how could he think that the crucified giant in Terminal Dogma was also Adam? There is no evidence in the show itself to help explain this. Not one, but two cases of apparent amnesia occur regarding Adam, one of which occurs only in the director's cut of episode 24. Since everything else that was changed for the director's cuts seems so carefully orchestrated, how could such a big error occur? I think I have come across some stuff that, when cross-referenced with other stuff, may show that this is not an error at all. First, we have the Classified Information file on Adam. It states the following: Reichu wrote: She was discovered at the South Pole by the Katsuragi Team, but, during the investigation, she blew apart and degenerated due to her S^2 Engine artificially going out of control.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:12 pm Wow. This is one of those situations were after reading what you posted, everything just kinda clicked into place. Though specific stuff about the Angels/Evas isn't exactly my forte I like what you have with this. (To be honest though, I was under the impression that Kaji KNEW the giant in Terminal Dogma was Lilith not Adam, but left Misato in the dark to protect her from finding out that information too soon/prevent Seele from hurting her.)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:13 pm Hmm... interesting idea. Seems rather solid.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:18 pm Was SEELE under the misapprehension that the giant being in Terminal Dogma was Adam? They certainly knew about Unit-01's origin...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:33 pm NAveryW wrote: Nope, the CI says that the Angels already existed before Adam was blown up.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:38 pm Reichu wrote: Insert tired arguments about how much soul-memory Adam would have retained versus Rei's bizarre amnesias.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:44 pm Reichu wrote: NAveryW wrote: Nope, the CI says that the Angels already existed before Adam was blown up.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 30, 2008 10:58 pm ...No that's just awkwardness in translation they cloned Adam, they say this a lot

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 30, 2008 11:04 pm The language in the CI does intimate towards "chunks" and "pieces", though the phrase "blown to bits" kept coming to mind as a possible English equivalent, which, despite how it would literally be read, doesn't really imply that there's anything meaningful left over. That's about as helpful as I can be; someone who knows what they're talking about would be more so. Also, no more tangents, peeps. I just split out one.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sat Aug 30, 2008 11:32 pm Wasn't he just manipulated? (side note: perhaps like the rest of the angels?)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 30, 2008 11:54 pm NAW has already sowed the seed of the monstrous episode 24 fanwank thread ; we shall now watch its demonic offspring grow and recreate the parent, complete with Jimbo/AEF bitch-slapping contests .

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sun Aug 31, 2008 12:05 am Sigh... I'll leave analyzing this for another time, 27 pages and relevant transcripts are a lot to go through. My current thought is that Seele manipulation is the easiest explanation...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 31, 2008 1:30 am For what it's worth, the page count is misleading; the thread eventually degenerates into aimless garbage. (This is the case for most threads, but, unlike most threads, this one actually got itself locked.) Actually, the whole thing might be garbage, but my ability to look at episode 24 critically was destroyed, so I'm not completely certain.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 31, 2008 5:44 am Delivering the embryo doesn't necessarily mean that they would know where it would end up. Maybe the surprise was just a simple thing about how they weren't expecting the way things were setup (contrary to their belief)...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 31, 2008 11:47 am V wrote: ...No that's just awkwardness in translation they cloned Adam, they say this a lot

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Aug 31, 2008 2:43 pm Excellent theory is excellent.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 31, 2008 6:34 pm NAveryW wrote: They are only clones in that they are copies.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 31, 2008 6:52 pm Reichu wrote: NAveryW wrote: They are only clones in that they are copies.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 31, 2008 7:01 pm They 'aren't just copies' because there's a "human will" inside. I.e., they're still "Adam copies", just with Lilin souls stuck in them, which is restated more clearly (albeit embedded in the most overall nonsensical monologue in the series) in 23. Full context always helps.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Sep 01, 2008 5:49 am A few things: -This is an old theory that I don't think has ever been fairly explored (it gets written off too easy for some reason). I think it was first brought up when someone mentions the Adam embryo is labeled "sample". Sample implies there's more. And if there's more, where is it and what is it? Is the Gendo/Adam hand embryo special? The only "specialty" I could ever ascribe to it is to assume that that embryo contains the S2 that has the capability of regenerating Adam. This fits in with the idea that Lilith somehow gets an S2 in EoE and uses its voodoo. -I think we can write off the Evas being copies of Adam yet also differing wildly in appearance as simply the inherent randomness and imperfection in the cloning process. We already know the process is imperfect since they're born without S2s, so I think physical differences are only natural. -This still does nothing to answer the Seele/Kaworu relationship, though I think I came to a breakthrough on that front: [Slight tangent] I've come to the conclusion that Seele obviously had the events of EoE planned. They mention it directly as soon as 23', especially the "returning Tokyo-3/Evas to their true forms. This had me confused until I remembered the true form of Tokyo-3 is Lilith's Egg. So what do they need? An explosion like we see in EoE. How is this relevant? One confusing line in 24' was the "It looks like (Seele) is trying to force our hand" from Gendo. I finally found a way to connect the above Seele knowledge to this line, which is given meaning in Misato's implication that if Kaworu gets too close, they'll have no choice but to self-destruct Nerv. This finally puts a major puzzle piece in place: Namely that sending Kaworu to Nerv was win/win for Seele. If they stop him, then all the Angels are out of the way in preparation for the events in EoE. If they can't stop him, perhaps they destroy Nerv, and there's nothing to stop Seele from an obstruction-free 3I/Instrumentality. Now, if someone can just figure out what was in it for Kaworu. I still stick with the theory that he thought Seele was giving him the keys to Earth.[/tangent] Reichu wrote: Also, this idea does nothing to alleviate that feeling in my gut that Kaworu shouldn't have been so daft as to mistake his previous body for a giant marshmallowy thing.

 Nerv was win/win for Seele. If they stop him, then all the Angels are out of the way in preparation for the events in EoE. If they can't stop him, perhaps they destroy Nerv, and there's nothing to stop Seele from an obstruction-free 3I/Instrumentality. Now, if someone can just figure out what was in it for Kaworu. I still stick with the theory that he thought Seele was giving him the keys to Earth.[/tangent] Reichu wrote: Also, this idea does nothing to alleviate that feeling in my gut that Kaworu shouldn't have been so daft as to mistake his previous body for a giant marshmallowy thing.
